title: Nomad Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Nomad CLI commands for managing workloads

commands.description: Start a Nomad agent in development mode
commands.scenario: Use when you need to quickly start a Nomad agent for testing purposes

commands.description: Run a job using a Nomad job file
commands.scenario: Use when you need to deploy a job defined in a Nomad job file

commands.description: Display the status of all jobs
commands.scenario: Use to check the current status of all running jobs in the cluster

commands.description: Stop a running job by ID
commands.scenario: Use when you need to stop a specific job that is currently running

commands.description: Get the status of a specific allocation
commands.scenario: Use to retrieve detailed information about a specific allocation

commands.description: List all server members in the cluster
commands.scenario: Use to view all the server nodes that are part of the Nomad cluster

commands.description: List the status of all client nodes
commands.scenario: Use to check the status and health of all client nodes in the cluster

commands.description: Evaluate a specific evaluation by ID
commands.scenario: Use to manually trigger an evaluation of a job or allocation

commands.description: Create a plan for a job without running it
commands.scenario: Use to preview the changes a job file will make before applying them

commands.description: Revert a job to a previous version
commands.scenario: Use to rollback a job to a previous stable version

commands.description: Display information about the local agent
commands.scenario: Use to get details about the configuration and status of the local Nomad agent

commands.description: Apply an ACL policy from a file
commands.scenario: Use to enforce access control policies using a predefined HCL file
